summary: First testing pass of the ported Printalaram Next.js frontend + FastAPI backend. Tested full e2e flows: homepage product grid, product detail page, live customization preview + photo upload, checkout (COD and simulated Razorpay), order-success page, shipping-returns page, mobile responsive nav. Wrote 16 pytest backend tests covering catalog, customizations (incl. upload URL reachability), orders, and payments (simulated Razorpay flow) - all passed.

minor.issue: orderNumber generated via str(uuid.uuid4().int)[:8] which is not cryptographically random distribution across digits and could theoretically collide/repeat leading zero-truncation quirks; low risk but consider using a proper counter or hash-based generator for guaranteed uniqueness at scale.

action_items: Known gap (already flagged by main agent): almost no data-testid attributes on interactive elements (buttons, inputs, links) across Header, product cards, customize form, checkout form, order-success. Recommend adding data-testid to: product card links, qty +/-/preset buttons, Customize Now/Buy Now buttons, customize form inputs (groom/bride/date/family/message/photo-upload), Save & Proceed button, checkout form fields, payment method radios, Place Order button, WhatsApp float button, hamburger menu button - for future automated regression testing.

Quantity presets on product page only show 50/100/250 (no explicit '10' preset button) though requirement mentioned 10/50/100/250 - the -/+ buttons do step by 10 down to a minimum of 10, so functionally reachable, just no dedicated '10 pcs' quick button. Minor/cosmetic, confirm if intentional.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 212-line file with all routes together - fine at current size but consider splitting into routers (products, customizations, orders, payments) if more endpoints are added later.
save_photo_from_base64 correctly avoids storing arbitrary file types via mimetypes.guess_extension; good practice.
MongoDB _id correctly excluded via projection {'_id': 0} in get_customization/get_order - good.
CORS_ORIGINS defaults to '*' which is fine for this preview env but should be restricted before any real production deploy.

No input validation limit on personalMessage server-side (frontend limits to 120 chars via slice, but backend CustomizationCreate accepts any length) - low risk since it's just text stored in Mongo, not rendered unsafely, but worth noting.
No rate limiting / abuse protection on /api/orders or /api/customizations - acceptable for MVP given no auth requirement, but flag for production hardening.
test_credentials: N/A - no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_-prefixed customizations and orders in MongoDB during testing (not cleaned up, low volume, safe to ignore since product/category data is static and unaffected).
context_for_next_testing_agent: This is first-pass testing of a freshly ported Next.js+FastAPI Printalaram app. All core flows work end-to-end: homepage (21 products) -> product detail -> customize (live preview + base64 photo upload -> /api/uploads/<uuid> reachable) -> checkout (COD flow opens WhatsApp deep link + redirects to order-success; Razorpay flow correctly falls back to simulated mode since RAZORPAY_KEY_ID is placeholder, marks order paid, redirects to order-success) -> order-success displays summary + WhatsApp button. Mobile hamburger nav and WhatsApp float button work at 390px. Shipping-returns static page renders all sections. No blocking bugs found. Main gap is the near-total absence of data-testid attributes (known/flagged by main agent already) - not a functional bug but will make future automated frontend testing harder/more brittle (relied on placeholder/text selectors this round, which worked but are less robust).
rca_of_the_issue: One transient Cloudflare 502 was observed on first COD checkout attempt when using page.context.expect_page() to catch the WhatsApp window.open() tab - likely a transient proxy/edge blip unrelated to app code, since backend logs showed the order POST succeeding and a retry of the identical flow (without waiting on expect_page) completed successfully with correct redirect to /order-success. Not flagged as an app bug.
